Same service, double the price? Check your Support at Home rates

With Australia’s biggest aged care reform in nearly three decades now live, thousands of older Australians are discovering changes they didn’t expect — from fees shifting, to services being repriced, to support levels being reassessed.

If you’re wondering whether you’re still with the right provider, you’re asking the right question.

Because under Support at Home, every dollar matters — and things like unclear communication, unexplained fees, or support plans that don’t reflect your needs can quickly add up.

The good news?

Switching providers is easier than most people think — most families transition in 2–4 weeks. And with service prices now varying by as much as 100%, choosing a better-value provider could mean twice as much care from the same budget.

You deserve care that feels transparent, fair, and truly centred around you 💚
New home care rules: here’s what’s changed.
Learn More
​
Active

copy_text:
Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

3. Early access to home

Major changes are coming to Home Care Packages from November 1, 2025 with the new Support at Home program.

1. 300,000 Additional packages to be released over the next 10 years with the waiting time for approval targeted to drop to under 3 months by July 2027. No more year long waiting lists!

2. No more package levels - instead you'll get a quarterly budget based on your individual needs, with 8 different funding classifications ranging from $11,000 to $78,000 per year.

3. Early access to home modifications and equipment - a separate upfront fund of up to $15,000 for assistive technology and home modifications, rather than having to save up your package funds.

4. Care management fees to be capped at 10% of the participants quarterly budget. 

While we’re excited about the much overdue changes to packages and waitlists we’re concerned that some providers will have to make up for lost care management revenue by increasing YOUR service fees. Because of this its more important then ever to be vigilant and understand your management fees AND hourly service fees.

We've seen service fees vary by up to 100% in the same region. Meaning that even though management fees might be lower, people still aren't getting as much care as they are entitled to.

We've built a free tool that compares providers by management fees and service fees. All you have to do is enter your postcode and your home care package level and we'll show you the best value for money providers in your area.

https://www.caringco.com.au/app/homecare
Don't set and forget your Home Care Package Provider
Learn More
​
Active

Personalised care without leaving home.
